title: The Coachman.
content:
There was a grand coachman,
        Who drove the Lord Mayor;
    And never drove less than
        A carriage and pair.

    He wore a red waistcoat,
        He carried a whip,
    And when the boys saw him,
        They shouted "Hip! hip!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Hunter delivers a powerful performance in "The Coachman."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
